summary: Mapped remaining Devpilot deliverable gaps from P8 current status, active TODO ledger, task-pull API/CLI code, and existing test surfaces. No speculative backlog was added.

source_backed_gaps.current_evidence: The CLI has bounded/forever foreground polling, optional heartbeat, default runner identity, pid-file lifecycle, startup failure summaries, and failure exit surfaces. The docs repeatedly keep background daemonization, process supervision, and full long-connection/runtime operation out of scope.
source_backed_gaps.next_action: Close the smallest real-agent runtime gap by making the foreground long-running agent profile operationally verifiable with current heartbeat/pid/summary surfaces, or mark production process-manager integration as an external blocker if it cannot be represented in repo-owned code/tests.

source_backed_gaps.current_evidence: API claim returns a sanitized task payload with lifecycle/terminal hints, CLI once can execute command steps, ack/progress and final ack cancellation are implemented, and finish writes terminal logs/result with fallback. The remaining gap is not another helper split by itself; it needs an end-to-end runtime proof across claim -> local command execution -> finish writeback, followed by defect fixes only if that proof fails.
source_backed_gaps.next_action: Run a local API/CLI terminal task-pull runtime scenario using the existing surfaces; if it exposes a product bug, fix inside the narrowed API/CLI terminal runtime window and rerun.

source_backed_gaps.current_evidence: Queue coordination preflight/readiness and runner identity/lock-owner differentiation exist, but docs still exclude complete multi-instance scheduling and coordination from the completed task-pull runtime slices.
source_backed_gaps.next_action: After S004/S005 prove the single-agent runtime, create a dedicated design/implementation slice for queue claim fairness, runner ownership recovery, and multi-runner scheduling semantics.

source_backed_gaps.current_evidence: Focused API/CLI task-pull Jest exists, supervisor endpoints are role-gated, and the Web execution-governance page consumes supervisor/job/lease APIs. S003 did not find a product-flow E2E gate proving a realistic permission path across API/Web/CLI surfaces.
source_backed_gaps.next_action: Include permission/product-flow E2E verification in the final deliverability gate; fix only verified defects.

source_backed_gaps.current_evidence: P8 current status still lists deeper production remote-orphan governance as a follow-up separate from task-pull runtime closure.
source_backed_gaps.next_action: After task-pull runtime deliverability gates, create a remote-orphan production governance slice if it remains in the deliverable scope.

rejected_speculation: No Prisma migration was inferred for S004/S005.
No Web UI redesign was inferred for S004/S005.
No server endpoint shape change was inferred before runtime verification proves it necessary.
No recursive worker or continuation thread is authorized by this gap map.
recommended_queue: S004: real-agent long-running foreground runtime/operability slice
S005: terminal runtime end-to-end verification-first slice
S006: final deliverability E2E and permission gate
S007 candidate: multi-instance coordination after single-agent runtime proof
S008 candidate: production remote-orphan governance after task-pull runtime proof
